figures.caption: Fig. 5 Partial derivative terms in ∂yE are ordered first by time and then by cell index. Blocks where time iteration k /∈ {ν − 1, ν} are 0.

Fig. 8 Plot of the level curves for the TTD for two different control variables, where the fixed parameters have the values defined in (26). In (a) and (b), the class is fixed, while in (c) and (d), the junction is fixed.

Fig. 7 Plot of the level curves for the TTT for two different control variables, where the fixed parameters have the values defined in (26). In (a) and (b), the class is fixed, while in (c) and (d), the junction is fixed.

Fig. 9 Results comparing the discrete adjoint gradient and the finite difference computation for a Matlab R2024a fmincon optimization with an sqp algorithm, with increasing values of a = log2 n, a = 0, . . . , 6, where n is the number of time-interval subdivisions.
Fig. 10 Plot of the Pareto front of the TTT against the TTD for different time-interval subdivisions n.
Fig. 6 Diagram of the test network showing the different roads and their associated parameters. Junctions e = 2, 3 diverge and junctions e = 4, 5 merge.

Fig. 11 Results comparing the total vehicle densities on roads 1, 2 and 6 of the network represented in Figure 6 for some of the points shown in Figure 10, together with the initial point (1439, 29865). Point (b) minimizes TTT over TTD, point (c) minimizes both functionals and point (d) minimizes TTD over TTT.
